arts.ttl: How Hull City's Yorkshire rivals have helped Plymouth Argyle prepare for crucial Championship game
arts.sum: Argyle have remained in the north of England since their 3-1 FA Cup defeat at Manchester City on Saturday night in a bid to help the players recover and prepare for what is arguably their biggest game of the season against the Tigers, rather than have to travel back to Devon and then come back again. Hull City's Yorkshire rivals, Huddersfield Town, have offered a helping hand to Plymouth Argyle in their preparations to take on the Tigers at the MKM Stadium tonight.

arts.ttl: Puerta starts: Hull City team news and predicted XI to face Plymouth Argyle
arts.sum: Hull City are back in action this evening with a home clash against Plymouth Argyle at the MKM… The post Puerta starts: Hull City team news and predicted XI to face Plymouth Argyle appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Houghton in: Plymouth Argyle team news and predicted XI to face Hull City
arts.sum: The latest Plymouth Argyle team news as Miron Muslic's side gear up to face Hull City in the Championship. The post Houghton in: Plymouth Argyle team news and predicted XI to face Hull City appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Ruben Selles embraces Hull City pressure as Championship strugglers seek to change narrative
arts.sum: Hull City head coach Ruben Selles believes there is no more pressure on Tuesday night's MKM Stadium meeting with Plymouth Argyle than any other game, saying the ambition must be to win every single time his team goes out to play. "Of course, it's a team that is in and around our position, and we would like to beat them, but it's just because we prepare the game in the same way as we would prepare any other game, we need to be focused on the things that we can do to just beat the opposition.

arts.ttl: Hull City man emerges as injury doubt for Plymouth Argyle clash
arts.sum: Hull City boss Ruben Selles has said Matt Crooks is a ‘doubt’ ahead of their clash against Plymouth… The post Hull City man emerges as injury doubt for Plymouth Argyle clash appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Plymouth doing things differently - and trying to change football
arts.sum: Plymouth Argyle’s players still have not returned home after their FA Cup tie against Manchester City on Saturday. The club’s commitment to make environmentally conscious travel choices means they are travelling on to play Hull on Tuesday evening.

arts.ttl: Nathan Ake breaks silence on foot injury as he reveals extent of blow after being forced off against Plymouth
arts.sum: However, the Dutch defender has endured a frustrating season, with Ake playing just 18 times in all competitions in 2024-25 following an injury-disrupted campaign.

arts.ttl: Inside the Hull City dressing room as Ruben Selles' team aim to take giant step towards safety
arts.sum: "I wouldn't say (it's squeaky bum time), I wouldn't say so no, even if it seems like that as an outsider looking in, but we've got to take it game by game and keep fighting and keep digging deep, to put points on the board. Three points for City would move the Tigers six points clear of the Pilgrims with 11 games of the season left to play, and in the process, secure a first home win of 2025 and just a third of the campaign so far.

arts.ttl: Time for Hull City to stand up and be counted in biggest home game of the season
arts.sum: The time is now for Hull City to grasp their chance to take a major step towards ensuring they're a Championship club next season, and not one languishing in the depths of League One. Just as they did last season when game after game went by and City failed to grasp a play-off place, if they're not careful, the same will happen this time round, but with far graver consequences.

arts.ttl: Mixed Hull City injury news as the Tigers prepare for vital Plymouth Argyle clash
arts.sum: Selles will also now be unable to call upon Crooks' fellow January arrival Eliot Mazato after he became the third Tigers player this season to suffer an ACL knee injury, which he picked up in last week's tepid 1-0 loss at Cardiff City, and loan signing Louie Barry who has returned to Aston Villa for treatment on a knee ligament injury he suffered in the success at Sunderland. Palmer has not played since coming off with an ankle problem in the defeat at the Jamaican's former club Coventry City before Christmas, but his return is a timely boost for City given the other injury concerns that have multiplied in the past 10 days.
